trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: COVID, cancer and shipwreck artifacts
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 10/27/25

urls.page_title: Humanities Scholars Program Fall Open House - Cornell
urls.page_summary: CU First-years and sophomores from ANY college on campus: Considering a major or minor in a humanities field? Seeking a community of scholars with diverse humanities interests? Interested in completing a senior research project on a topic of your ...
urls.page_title: Cornell team finds new way to cut cancer’s lipid lifeline | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Researchers have uncovered molecules that can preserve crucial cellular processes while blocking malignant proteins, indicating a new approach to fighting cancer.
urls.page_title: Objects from famous shipwreck tell deeper stories
urls.page_summary: La Pérouse’s expedition, wrecked in 1788, was intended to rival those of British explorer Captain James Cook and to bring the French renown in scientific knowledge. Through the visual materials related to the voyage and its wreck, Kelly Presutti t...
urls.page_title: 2025 Cornell Neurotech Mong Family Foundation Symposium - Cornell
urls.page_summary:
Featuring Mala Murthy and H. Sebastian Seung
Mala Murthy Karol and Marnie Marcin ’96 Professor of Neuroscience; and Director, Princeton Neuroscience Institute, Princeton University

urls.page_title: Event considers political lessons learned from COVID
urls.page_summary: A Nov. 13 event sponsored by the College of Arts & Sciences will feature reflections on the political and social context and consequences of the Covid epidemic.

An A.D. White Professor-at-Large keynote event
CHASING TIME (2024) Tuesday, October 28 6:00pm · Cornell Cinema 40 min. short documentary, followed by Q&A with James Balog, A.D. White Professor-at-Large Get your free tickets at cornellcinema.event...
urls.page_title: Citizen Scholar: Public Engagement for Social Scientists - Cornell
urls.page_summary: What is the role of professional scholars in civic life? How and why should academics seek to reach audiences beyond their disciplines and institutions? Must there be tension between advancing along an academic career path and taking part in publi...
